nodes.label: AI as scientific reference tool for factual identification
nodes.label_negative: AI as creative partner in problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: AI is a reference for me like a deeper google search not a creative partner

nodes.label: AI use for quick, bounded tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI use for extended, open-ended inquiry
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to just ask the question and move on without further questions

nodes.label: Preserving hands-on, intuitive work as core of job
nodes.grounding_span: I do prefer generally to figure most things out myself

nodes.label: AI as separate from creative process
nodes.grounding_span: AI feels wholly separate from that process

nodes.label: AI stigma in environmentally-oriented workplace
nodes.grounding_span: it is slightly taboo to use AI

nodes.label: Hands-on experiential knowledge and skill
nodes.grounding_span: Working with my hands is very important to me

nodes.label: Creative problem-solving and curiosity
nodes.grounding_span: the curiosity to find a solution is the most stimulating part of the job

nodes.label: Efficiency in factual tasks
nodes.grounding_span: for the sake of efficiency I will sometimes request help from AI

nodes.label: Environmental authenticity and values
nodes.grounding_span: we do such physical labor and are very environmentally based

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for precise identification
Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want AI to infringe anymore on the process of problem solving

nodes.label: Ambivalence about future AI use due to context
nodes.grounding_span: it's way more gray than just using AI or not and my habits could definitely change

nodes.label: Preference for single-query, reference-style interaction
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to just ask the question and move on without further questions

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting between AI and human work
nodes.grounding_span: I would rarely integrate AI into that problem-solving process
